interactions.summary: Sneako asks when Greater Israel can be seen. Jiang answers that Netanyahu and Hamas have a long mutually useful relationship, October 7 kept Netanyahu in power, Greater Israel is openly discussed, and the Iran war clears regional obstacles until U.S. retreat allows Israel to pursue the project; he calls Gulf states mirages and caste societies.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Sneako asks or frames the issue; Jiang answers in the main analytic voice where present.

speaker_notes.note: Machine diarization is consistent in this packet: SPEAKER_00 is Sneako/interviewer and SPEAKER_01 is Jiang, except where intro/outro material is host-only.

claims.claim: Jiang argues Hamas and Netanyahu have historically worked well together, with Hamas violence helping Netanyahu politically in 1996 and October 7 rescuing him from corruption and judicial-crisis pressure.
He says the Greater Israel project and Third Temple are the real end goal, with the Iran war drawing in and weakening GCC states and Turkey until the United States retreats from the Middle East.
He describes Gulf states such as Dubai/UAE as mirages or caste systems built on expatriate labor and third-world workers treated as slaves.

signature_moments.moment: Greater Israel is tied to a redemptive script: for redemption to happen, history has to be played out.
signature_moments.source_phrase: for this redemption to happen, you need to play out history
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This links geopolitical expansion to sacred chronology.

signature_moments.moment: Dubai becomes a mirage: a fantasy land in the desert that Jiang says may not be around by September.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It gives the Gulf-state diagnosis a memorable physical image.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang’s term for a territorial-redemptive project he says is openly discussed in Israel and advanced through the Iran war.
